items.title: India’s Fuel Exports Plunge in May as Domestic Needs Prioritized
items.summary: India’s fuel exports tumbled to the lowest level in nearly four years in May as a push to ensure domestic supplies during the Iran war shock along with refinery maintenance curbed overseas flows.

items.title: Incoming Tepco Chair Open to Alliance Partners Outside of Japan
items.summary: The incoming chairman of Tokyo Electric Power Co. said he is open to forming alliances with companies outside of Japan as the nation’s largest utility seeks to restructure its operations and fund the massive cost of cleaning up the Fukushima nuclear disaster.
